content.full_text:
CITY OF

Oxnard

CITY ATTORNEY • 300 W. THIRD STREET • OXNARD, CALIFORNIA 93030 • (805) 984-4601 FAX (805) 486-9462

GARY L. GILLIG, CITY ATTORNEY

April 8, 1992

Deanna Stone, Esq.
Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
Sacramento, Ca. 95814

Dear Ms. Stone:

As we recently discussed by telephone, the Oxnard City Council
asked the Oxnard City Attorney Department to request from the
Fair Political Practices Commission ("the Commission") formal
written advice as to whether a council member should have
disqualified himself on grounds of conflict of interest from
voting on a certain issue arising in connection with the City's
adoption of a new General Plan. If a conflict of interest
existed, the City Council is also interested in what effect that
conflict has on the vote on the issue and on the City's
subsequent adoption of the General Plan.

The vote in question was taken on August 31, 1990, and the
General Plan was adopted on October 9, 1990. Prior to
participating in the vote in question, the councilmember
requested verbal advice from the City Attorney, who concluded
that the councilmember was not disqualified from voting.

The council member's residence is located within 300 feet of the
eastern boundary of an undeveloped area of approximately 750
acres, which is located outside the City's sphere of influence
but within the City's planning area. The area is outlined in
orange on the attached map, and the location of the council
member's residence is shown with a blue dot.

Under the City's previous General Plan, the area was designated
Open Space, which allowed no development other than agriculture
and a few other low-intensity uses. The area was not included in
any greenbelt agreement between the City and any other entity.
"Greenbelt agreements" have been entered into between the City
and neighboring cities and the County of Ventura with regard to
areas in which such entities share interests. Such agreements
restrict the acceptable uses for the areas subject to the
agreement.


On August 31, 1990 the City Council discussed the appropriate
designation for the area and whether the area should be included
in a greenbelt agreement between the City and the County of
Ventura. The council member in question moved that the new
General Plan designate that portion of the area north of Gonzales
Road (the black line on the map just south of the council
member's home) as Agricultural Planning Reserve ("AG/PR") for ten
years, after which the designation would be re-evaluated, and
that the area not be included in any greenbelt agreement. The
designation of AG/PR allows almost the same uses as the previous
designation as Open Space.

The motion carried by a 3-2 vote of the City Council. Without
the vote of the council member in question, the motion would have
failed.

The remainder of the area was also designated AG/PR.

Subsection (b)(8)(A) of section 18329 of Title 2 of the
California Code of Regulations, provides that the Commission "may
decline to give formal written advice" if the requestor "is
seeking advice relating to past conduct."

As the advice sought relates to past conduct, I asked whether the
Commission would provide formal written advice in this matter.
You told me that the Commission's practice is not to provide
formal written advice relating to past conduct and asked that I
send this letter if I wished written confirmation of the
Commission's practice as applied to this request.

Would the Commission's practice differ because the City Council
seeks advice not only as to whether a council member should
have disqualified himself from the matter discussed and voted on
on August 31, 1990, but, if so, also as to the effect of such
failure to disqualify on the validity of the vote taken and the
Council's subsequent adoption of a General Plan based in part on
the outcome of that vote? Although the votes taken relate to
past conduct, the present effect of the votes taken is also part
of the advice requested.

Very truly yours,

Paula Kimbrell
Paula Kimbrell
Assistant City Attorney

cc: City Council


BAILARD
LANDFILL

AG/PR

AG/PR

AG/PR

OXNARD AIRPORT
